title: Add --tag filter to list command
author: dave
body: Closes #4. Adds a --tag option to `taskflow list` wired to Query(tag=...).
diff:
@@ cmd_list @@
-def cmd_list(store: Store, overdue: bool) -> list[str]:
+def cmd_list(store: Store, overdue: bool, tag: str | None = None) -> list[str]:
     tasks = store.load_tasks()
-    if overdue:
-        tasks = run_query(tasks, Query(done=False, overdue=True))
+    q = Query(done=False if overdue else None, overdue=overdue, tag=tag)
+    tasks = run_query(tasks, q)
review_targets: Setting done=False whenever overdue is passed changes existing behavior: a plain `list` (no flags) used to show done tasks too; this PR's Query(done=None) path is fine but the overdue branch now hides completed tasks — confirm that's intended.
No test added for the --tag path.
What happens with `--tag` and `--overdue` together? The composition isn't covered by a test.
